title: Bounded Public Mutation-Seam Hosted Deployment And Public PM Live-Data Proof
objective: Execute the smallest truthful hosted follow-through after Packet 030 by publishing the scaffold, attempting the public mutation-seam host bring-up, and only continuing to the operations-web rebuild or public PM live-data proof if the hosted seam can actually be reached.
decision_rationale: Packet 030 only landed the repo-owned host and ingress contract; it explicitly did not prove that `https://mutation-seam.apexpowerops.com` already existed or that public PM live-data was ready.

The smallest truthful next move was to publish the scaffold to `origin/clean-main`, probe the intended public seam host, and continue into the Vercel rebuild only if the public seam could actually be deployed and reached.

A hosted deployment attempt from this environment depends on a Render account surface, while the follow-on operations-web rebuild depends on Vercel access. Those are separable control points and needed to be tested independently.

Once the public seam host failed DNS resolution and no usable Render authentication surface was evidenced in the browser session, local shell, or config directories, continuing to claim hosted deployment progress would have been false.
execution_summary: Published the Packet 027 through Packet 030 authority burst plus the Packet 030 mutation-seam ingress scaffold to `origin/clean-main` in commit `fe0cb168e0a751b8364cfe72ad74283088cc824e` with message `Publish Olares mutation-seam ingress scaffold`.

Ran `python apps/mutation-seam/scripts/smoke_deployed_mutation_seam.py --base-url https://mutation-seam.apexpowerops.com` and confirmed the intended public seam host does not yet resolve, failing with `getaddrinfo failed` before any route-level validation could begin.

Inspected the Render control surface and confirmed `https://dashboard.render.com/` lands on the login page; GitHub OAuth redirects to `github.com/login`, so there is no usable authenticated Render browser session in this environment.

Checked the local shell for Render access and found no `render` CLI, no Render config footprint, and no Render environment variables, while `npx vercel whoami` succeeded and proved the Vercel side remains credentialed for the later operations-web step.

validation.publication: git commit -m 'Publish Olares mutation-seam ingress scaffold'; git push origin clean-main -> PASS (commit fe0cb168e0a751b8364cfe72ad74283088cc824e)
validation.public_host_baseline: python apps/mutation-seam/scripts/smoke_deployed_mutation_seam.py --base-url https://mutation-seam.apexpowerops.com -> FAIL (Failed to reach /health: getaddrinfo failed)
validation.render_browser_access: https://dashboard.render.com/ -> login required; GitHub OAuth redirects to github.com/login
validation.render_local_access_surface: Get-Command render -> not found; Test-Path ~/.config/render, ~/.render, ~/AppData/Roaming/render -> all False
validation.vercel_cli_access: npx vercel whoami -> Logged in as jasonlswenson-sys; Active team: jasonlswenson-sys-projects

next_packet_candidate: Olares Dev Residency 032 - Credentialed Public Mutation-Seam Deployment And Public PM Live-Data Proof
closure_note: Executed 2026-05-06 as a bounded hosted follow-through attempt. Packet 031 truthfully closes the publish-and-probe slice, proves that `mutation-seam.apexpowerops.com` is not yet deployed, and records that the remaining blocker is missing Render account access from this environment. It does not claim that the public seam host was created, that `operations-web` was rebuilt with `MUTATION_SEAM_BASE_URL`, or that public PM live-data proof passed.
next_action: Resume with usable Render credentials or an authenticated Render session that can create or configure the public `mutation-seam` service, validate it with the deployed seam smoke, then use the already-authenticated Vercel CLI to rebuild and promote `operations-web` with `MUTATION_SEAM_BASE_URL` pointed at that host before rerunning public PM live-data proof.
